subject: Rings
method: full human/LLM read of transcripts 053 (Act 1) + 054 (Act 2) + 055 (Act 3), not the heuristic
note: All 63 tiers confirmed against the transcripts. You equip TWO rings, so the slot rewards situational/synergy picks alongside raw stats. Auto-caption renderings: Ring of Feywild Sparks='ring of fay wild sparks', The Sparkswall='the sparks wall', Djinni Ring='jinny ring', Firzu's='fu's', Fetish of Callarduran Smoothhands='cerin smooth hands', After Death Do Us Part='afterdeath do us part'.

Caustic Band.why: +2 acid damage on every weapon attack (melee or ranged) — acid is almost never resisted, so it's near-universal bonus damage available early; goes on whoever makes the most attacks. Flat (not a die), so it isn't doubled on crits.

Crusher's Ring.why: +10ft movement, stacking with Longstrider — mobility is one of the best stats in honour mode (reach enemies, kite, escape losing fights), and every character benefits, so it's a near-universal early pick.

Ring of Evasion.why: Reaction to auto-succeed a failed Dexterity save (Gale-origin only) — halve every Fireball and dodge restraint effects at will; a powerful, very early defensive panic button that sticks around a long time.

Ring of Flinging.why: +1d4 damage on thrown attacks — huge for a throwing-weapon build (applied many times a round) and useful on any melee character carrying backup throwables; boosts one of the game's best strategies.

Ring of Protection.why: +1 AC and +1 to all saves from a half-slot — premium defensive stats every character wants; put it on your lowest-AC party member to raise the party's floor.

Shapeshifter's Boon Ring.why: +1d4 to ALL ability checks while disguised/shapeshifted — basically a second, permanent Guidance (stacks with it) if you walk around in Disguise Self; a massive boost to everything you do out of combat.

Strange Conduit Ring.why: +1d4 psychic on every weapon attack while concentrating on a spell (trivially maintained with a day-long concentration ritual) — even better than the Caustic Band since the die doubles on crits; a top damage ring all game.

The Whispering Promise.why: Bless (the spell) on anyone you heal — including yourself, and triggerable by thrown potions; near-permanent +1d4 to attacks and saves across the party, the core of healing builds and great even without one.

Callous Glow Ring.why: +2 radiant damage to any illuminated enemy, per damage instance — easily +10/+20 a round on multi-hit attackers, and being radiant it stacks Radiating Orb; one of the most universally contested items in the game.

Coruscation Ring.why: Two stacks of Radiating Orb on spell damage (bugged to ignore the light requirement) — the engine of radiating-orb builds (especially with Spirit Guardians), and a free attack-roll debuff for any damage caster.

Risky Ring.why: Advantage on ALL attacks (and disadvantage on all saves) — permanent free advantage is enormous (more hits, more crits); the save downside is real but offset by going first, killing fast, or save-advantage gear.

Shadow Cloaked Ring.why: +1d4 damage to obscured enemies on weapon AND unarmed attacks — a die (so it crit-doubles) of bonus damage that's easy to maintain in darkness; can't combine with the Callous Glow Ring (lit vs obscured).

Snowburst Ring.why: Creates a 15ft ice surface on any cold damage — free prone-inducing, turn-ending ice under enemies (often better than a stun); best-in-slot for any cold-damage character, with prone-immunity needed for melee use.

Band of the Mystic Scoundrel.why: Cast an illusion/enchantment spell as a bonus action after a weapon hit — effectively permanent Haste for a hybrid attacker/caster (attack, then Command/Hypnotic Pattern), and it pairs perfectly with Arcane Acuity.

Crypt Lord Ring.why: Create Undead (a level-6 summon, a mummy) once per long rest — a free 6th-level spell slot and a durable extra body; the only non-wizard way to get the spell, great in summon/necromancer parties.

Ring of Feywild Sparks.why: Guarantees wild-magic procs under Tides of Chaos for a wild sorcerer, but the real draw is an UNLISTED +1 spell save DC for any caster — a save-DC boost from the ring slot, which is otherwise rare; best-in-slot for offensive casters.

Ring of Regeneration.why: 1d4 healing at the start of each combat turn — minor alone, but it triggers every on-heal effect each turn (permanent Bless via Whispering Promise, permanent Blade Ward via Reviving Hands); a powerful synergy engine.

Bracing Band.why: +1 AC for a turn whenever you displace an enemy — excellent on a warlock with Repelling Blast (near-constant AC) and decent for any character whose best bonus action is a shove; passive +1 AC rings are stronger, though.

Ring of Absolute Force.why: +1 thunder damage whenever you deal thunder damage (plus Thunderwave once/rest) — the extra instance can re-trigger reverberation/radiating orb, so it's strong for thunder-based reverberation builds and Tempest clerics.

Ring of Arcane Synergy.why: Arcane Synergy (add your spellcasting modifier to weapon attacks) on dealing cantrip damage — a big damage boost for pact-of-the-blade warlocks, but the Diadem of Arcane Synergy triggers more easily, so this is the backup/second copy.

Ring of Salving.why: +2 HP to every heal you give another creature — strong for a dedicated healer (life cleric) and it boosts thrown-potion efficiency; not mandatory, since many healing builds care about the on-heal buffs more than the numbers.

Smuggler's Ring.why: +2 stealth and +2 Sleight of Hand (-1 Charisma) — a large out-of-combat boost to open almost any lock and to greater-invisibility stealth; swap it off a Bard/warlock before dialogue, but worth carrying all game.

The Sparkswall.why: Lightning resistance and immunity to electrocution — pairs with the Watersparkers boots (stand in electrified water for free lightning charges each turn without taking the damage), the core of lightning-charge builds; also a swap-in resistance.

Eversight Ring.why: Blind immunity — lets a non-warlock fight in their own Darkness/Fog Cloud (immune to ranged attacks, melee enemies blinded) and even shoot INTO darkness clouds; mandatory for darkness parties, situational otherwise.

Killer's Sweetheart.why: Your next weapon attack after a kill is a guaranteed crit (once/rest) — you control it by finishing a low-HP enemy, then landing a guaranteed-crit Smite/sneak attack; very strong for one encounter a day on a big-dice build.

Ring of Spiteful Thunder.why: Thunder damage to a reverberating enemy forces a con save or Dazed (no reactions) — basically free value for a thunder reverberation build (which already deals thunder), and it applies another condition to stack.

Fetish of Callarduran Smoothhands.why: Invisibility once per long rest — saves a slot/potion for the strong out-of-combat and pre-combat-positioning uses of invisibility; only one cast a day, so a swap-in utility.

Guiding Light.why: Light at will — saves a caster's cantrip slot (or carrying torches) on a universally-wanted utility effect; minor but genuinely handy, especially early.

Mage's Friend.why: +1 Arcana and +1 Religion — unlike Nature/Survival these are heavily-used dialogue skills with some big mechanical checks (esp. an Act 3 religion check), so it's a valuable swap-in for conversations.

Ring of Poison Resistance.why: Poison resistance — a relatively rare damage type, but resistances are always powerful, so a worthwhile swap-in for the few poison-heavy fights (e.g. Bhaal-adjacent); slightly generous B for the principle.

Ring of Geniality.why: +1 persuasion and +1 deception — persuasion is a high-value skill and there are important Act 2 persuasion checks (skipping bosses), so a useful dialogue swap-in; a small but real bonus.

Poisoner's Ring.why: Make up to three enemies poison-vulnerable (DC14 con, no combat start needed) — niche, but with Cloud Kill it doubles a huge poison-damage tick (effectively ~30d8 across three foes); a real build-around for poison casters.

Ring of Mental Inhibition.why: Mental Fatigue (-mental saves) when an enemy fails a save vs your spell/action — a poor-man's Arcane Acuity for landing Hold Person; hot-fixed to not work with walls/clouds/weapon riders, so it needs a specific stun-then-cast build.

Ring of Shadows.why: Pass Without Trace (+10 stealth) once/rest — basically mandatory for a greater-invisibility stealth-archer (higher stealth = more attacks before enemies react), niche otherwise.

Shadow Blade Ring.why: Bonus-action summon a 2d8 psychic shortsword — high base damage but no enhancement (lower hit chance), concentration-dependent, and its advantage-vs-obscured is bugged off; good built around psychic vulnerability (Resonance Stone).

Shifting Corpus Ring.why: Invisibility and Blur, each once/long rest — saves a level-2 slot on two useful utility/defensive spells; handy to carry, not game-changing.

True Love's Caress.why: Half of the Warding Bond pair (worn by the protected character) — lets a non-cleric cast Warding Bond, great on a high-HP barbarian or damage-reducing wizard, but the pair costs two ring slots in your active party.

True Love's Embrace.why: The other half of the Warding Bond pair (the caster) — same verdict: a strong effect (stacks toward quarter damage) but spending two precious ring slots on a level-3 spell is a real cost (better via a camp caster).

Burnished Ring.why: Paralyzing Ray once/rest — like a no-concentration Hold Person that targets Constitution and works on non-humanoids/undead; set it up with reverberation/bleed to land it on enemies you otherwise couldn't paralyze.

Djinni Ring.why: Mage Hand once per short rest — Mage Hand is itself once/rest, so this genuinely extends its use across more fights (throw water/potions for free actions); strong for solo/Mage-Hand-abuse builds, low-end B by Act 3.

Ring of Truthfulness.why: Advantage on Insight checks — valuable because Insight is a hidden roll you can't boost reactively, but you get it in Act 3 when few important Insight checks remain; a dialogue swap-in.

Explorer's Ring.why: +1 Nature and +1 Survival — two of the least-useful skills (failed Survival digs still work), so only a niche swap-in for a known check; nice for the click-saving of high Survival.

Ring of Elemental Infusion.why: +1d4 elemental damage on your next weapon attack after dealing elemental spell damage — needs the rare caster-who-also-weapon-attacks, and only fires ~every other turn; extra damage, but hard to leverage.

Ring of Jumping.why: Enhanced Leap once per short rest — duplicates a ritual spell most parties already have for free; only useful for an oddly-composed party that lacks the spell.

Spurred Band.why: One turn of momentum (+5ft) each turn you're at/below 50% HP — too small to justify sitting at half HP; its only real use is maintaining max momentum alongside another momentum source on a low-HP build.

Firzu's Ring of Trading.why: +1 deception (proficiency-coded, so +2 with expertise) — strictly worse than the Ring of Geniality (which also gives persuasion), so rarely worth carrying.

Ring of Free Action.why: Permanent Freedom of Movement (no difficult-terrain slow, immune to paralysis/restraint) — but it lacks the spell's stun-break panic use and doesn't stop Spike Growth damage; a situational swap-in for hold-person/plant-growth fights.

Ring of Self Immolation.why: Bonus action to gain Heat (set yourself on fire), dumped as bonus fire damage on a later Fireball — only worthwhile with other Heat sources; otherwise the Callous Glow Ring's constant +2 dominates it.

Ring of Twilight.why: +1 AC while obscured — strictly worse than the Ring of Protection, and conditional +1 AC isn't enough for the precious, competitive ring slot in Act 2.

After Death Do Us Part.why: Revive at half HP once/rest, then become uncontrollable (currently bugged to skip the downside, so just a free extra life) — a purely defensive panic button; useful for solo play (prevents game over), but better to not go down at all.

Ring of Blink.why: Blink once per long rest — a 50% chance to be untargetable each turn; too unreliable to plan around in a party (enemies just hit someone else), but genuinely good in solo play (removes ~half of enemy turns).

Till Death Do Us Part.why: Beacon of Hope once/rest (advantage on Wisdom saves, maximized healing) — useful in wisdom-save-heavy fights, but Heroes' Feast (which you usually have by Act 3) gives party-wide Wisdom-save advantage; niche for parties without it.

Ring.why: Non-magical rings have no effect — light and worth selling to merchants, but never worth equipping.

Mattis's Rings.why: Joke 'rings of resistance to ants' etc. from the Tiefling kid — Easter eggs with no mechanical effect; the author played whole runs wearing them and found nothing.

Ring of Colour Spray.why: Color Spray once/rest — a one-turn no-save blind, but you want longer blinds, and no-save Fog Cloud (also a free at-will item) does it far better; possibly the most useless magic item in the game.

Ring of Mind Shielding.why: Advantage on saves vs Charmed — a very narrow effect (and you get it after the harpies, the main early charm threat); Protection from Evil and Good just makes you immune, so no reason to use it.

Tarnished Ring.why: A little crafting mini-quest produces a ring with no actual mechanical effect (cut content or a data error) — D for its (non-existent) effect.

Family Ring.why: +2 to death saving throws — you should rarely be rolling death saves, and revivify/resurrection is cheap, so there's no reason to equip it; a flavor item.

Ring of Exalted Marrow.why: A melee paralysis (unboostable DC13 con) and an undead-only Command, each once/rest — the DC is too low to rely on and the spells are weak for Act 2; great name, mediocre effect.

Guild Ring.why: A quest ring with no mechanical effect — only 'useful' as Gale food if console-spawned in Act 1.

Keepsake Ring.why: Dominate Beast once/rest — a poor spell with few valid targets, concentration-dependent and breakable; there are almost no beast fights left by the time you get it in Act 3.

Orphic Ring.why: Confusion (upcast to 5) once/rest — only ~50% reliable and unpredictable, so a situational backup at best; not worth keeping equipped in advance when scrolls of Confusion are plentiful.

Seemingly Gleaming Ring.why: +1 Charisma saves but -1 Wisdom saves — you almost never make Charisma saves and Wisdom saves are the most dangerous, so it actively makes your character worse; an F-tier effect if the list had one.
